justifications.temporal_understanding: The agent fundamentally failed to understand the temporal expression, interpreting "tomorrow in the afternoon between 14 and 16" as June 2nd, 2026, when the conversation date was June 6th, 2026.
justifications.negotiation_effectiveness: The agent proposed alternatives but needed an extra turn to offer a different day after the initial time slots for "tomorrow" were unavailable.
justifications.conversational_efficiency: The agent made two unnecessary exchanges due to its initial misinterpretation of the date.
justifications.customer_experience: The interaction was workable but tiring due to the agent's initial misinterpretation of the date, requiring the user to clarify and re-state preferences.
justifications.recovery_quality: The agent recovered by offering to check a different day, but the initial misinterpretation of "tomorrow" indicates a loss of context regarding the current date.
